---
title: "ImPRESS Recurrent glioblastome Statistical Analysis Plan"
subtitle: "Draft"
author: "ImPRESS Statistical Team"
date: "`r format(Sys.Date(), '%d %B %Y')`"
output:
  word_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    reference_docx: "SAP_CoreBWE_v005.docx"
---

# Introduction

This Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) describes the analyses for the  Imaging Perfusion Restrictions from Extracellular Solid Stress (ImPRESS) study, a prospective stepped-wedge, randomized, assessor-blinded, dose-finding trial evaluating losartan as an adjunct to standard of care. This SAP is specific to the recurrent glioblastoma cohort.

This SAP covers clinical efficacy, safety and quality-of-life endpoints for the recurrent glioblastoma cohort, and specifies analysis populations, derivations, and statistical methods.

The SAP is based on Protocol Version 7.0 (17-Dec-2024). Unless otherwise stated, analyses follow the protocol; any deviations will be documented in Section 10.


# Objectives, Endpoints, and Estimands

## Study Objectives

Primary study objective:
To assess the dose-response relationship of the angiotensin II receptor inhibitor losartan on perfusion and mechanical solid stress in brain tumors.

Key secondary objectives:

- To assess the dose-response relationship of losartan on conventional and experimental radiographic characteristics in patients with glioblastoma and metastases to the brain.
- To assess the dose-response relationship of losartan as add-on to standard treatment on clinical or patient-reported endpoints in patients with glioblastoma and metastases to the brain.
- To assess the safety of losartan treatment in patients with glioblastoma and metastases to the brain.

## Endpoints

### Primary endpoints:

- Change from baseline in relative cerebral blood flow (rCBF) measured by perfusion MRI.
- Change from baseline in a radiographic biomarker representative of relative solid stress measured by MR Elastography.

### Secondary endpoints:

- Change from baseline in experimental radiographic biomarkers from MRI (including size of enhancing tumor, edema, perfusion MRI, diffusion MRI, and MR Elastography parameters).
- Change from baseline in quality of life using EORTC QLQ-C30 and QLQ-BN20.
- Steroid dosage and use.
- Blood pressure.
- Experimental biomarkers from blood/serum (e.g., angiogenic, inflammatory, and extracellular matrix markers).

### Time-to-event endpoints:

- Six-month progression-free survival (6M-PFS) at Day +180 (+7 days).
- Progression-free survival (PFS) up to 24 months (+7 days).
- Overall survival (OS) at 12 months (+7 days) and 24 months (+7 days).
- Overall survival (OS) up to 24 months.

### Safety endpoints:

- Blood pressure.
- Adverse events (AEs), serious adverse events (SAEs), and treatment-emergent adverse events (TEAEs).
- Laboratory parameters (if collected).

## Estimands

Placeholder: Define estimands for key endpoints, aligned with protocol.

# General Considerations

## Decision Criteria / Statistical Hypotheses

The ImPRESS study is a phase 2 trial designed to explore the dose-response relationship of losartan on primarily imaging-based biomarkers in patients with brain tumors. Thus, it is not a confirmatory study, and all hypothesis testing should be considered supportive and not confirmatory.  

The null hypothesis for each of the two primary endpoints is that there is no dose-response relationship across the planned dose levels (0, 25, 50, 100 mg). 

Unless otherwise stated, statistical tests are two-sided with a nominal 5% significance level and 95% confidence intervals.

## Multiplicity Adjustment

Multiplicity control for the primary MRI endpoints is addressed by the MCP-Mod framework as described in the Statistical Methods section. For secondary and other exploratory endpoints, no multiplicity adjustments are planned and p-values are considered descriptive.

## Impact of Intercurrent Events Strategies

Intercurrent events are handled according to estimand intent:

- For time-to-event endpoints, death is treated as an event for PFS and as the event for OS. Other intercurrent events (e.g., treatment discontinuation) do not alter the event definition; patients are censored at last contact.
- For longitudinal endpoints, all observed data are included as long as measurements are recorded; missing data are handled as described below.

## Handling of Missing Data

For continuous and categorical longitudinal endpoints, analyses use mixed models or generalized mixed models that are valid under a Missing At Random (MAR) assumption. No imputation is planned for the primary analyses.

For time-to-event endpoints, subjects without an event are censored at the last contact date. For milestone endpoints (6-month PFS, 12- and 24-month OS), follow-up is administratively censored at the milestone time.

# Study Design and Analysis Populations

## Study Design Summary

This is an individual stepped-wedge, randomized, assessor-blinded, dose-finding single-center trial on three indications: recurrent glioblastoma, newly diagnosed glioblastoma, and brain metastases. The analyses in this SAP are restricted to the recurrent glioblastoma cohort. Participants are assigned to cohorts by randomization and receive losartan within approved dose ranges (25 mg, 50 mg, 100 mg), aligned with standard-of-care treatment cycles.

Follow-up for PFS and OS is up to 24 months.

## Analysis Populations

All analyses in this SAP are restricted to the recurrent glioblastoma cohort.

- **Full Analysis Set (FAS)**: all randomized subjects in the recurrent glioblastoma cohort with at least one post-baseline assessment for the endpoint of interest; analyzed according to assigned treatment.
- **Safety Set (SS)**: all subjects who received at least one dose of losartan; analyzed according to treatment received.
- **Per-Protocol Set (PP)**: subjects in the FAS without major protocol deviations affecting the primary endpoint (if defined); used for sensitivity analyses.

# Data Handling and Derived Variables

## Data Sources

Analyses are based on ADaM datasets derived from the Viedoc export. Key analysis datasets include:

- **ADSL**: subject-level analysis data.
- **ADMRI**: MRI endpoints.
- **ADEFF**: neurologic performance, QoL, steroids, and RANO outcomes.
- **ADTTE**: time-to-event endpoints.

## Derived Variables

Baseline is defined as the last non-missing assessment on or before randomization. Change from baseline is calculated as post-baseline value minus baseline.

Time-to-event variables use randomization date as the start date. Progression is derived from the DP dataset (`dpdat`), death and last contact from the SA dataset (`sadtdat`, `saendat`).

# Statistical Methods

## General Methods

All analyses are conducted in R. Continuous outcomes are summarized by mean, standard deviation, median, and range. Categorical outcomes are summarized by counts and percentages. Model-based estimates are presented with 95% confidence intervals.

## Primary Efficacy Analyses (MRI)

Primary MRI endpoints (relative cerebral blood flow and relative solid stress) are analyzed using the MCP-Mod framework to assess dose-response relationships across the planned dose levels. Candidate models include linear, Emax, sigmoid Emax, and linear-in-log-dose. The MCP step uses optimal contrasts derived from model-based estimates; if a dose-response signal is detected, the Mod step fits candidate models and reports model fit and ED estimates.

## Other MRI Endpoints

Other MRI endpoints are summarized using mixed models for change from baseline at early timepoints. Model-based estimates by dose with 95% confidence intervals and contrasts versus 0 mg are reported.

## Neurologic Performance

KPS, ECOG, and NANO scores are analyzed with mixed models for change from baseline. Early timepoints and later follow-up timepoints are modeled separately when applicable, and estimates are presented by dose.

## Quality of Life Outcomes

QoL outcomes (QLQ-C30 and QLQ-BN20) are analyzed using mixed models for change from baseline at later visits where treatment groups remain distinct. Estimates and contrasts are reported with 95% confidence intervals.

## Steroid Use

Steroid use is analyzed with generalized mixed models for early visits and generalized linear models for later visits. Population-averaged risks by dose and risk differences versus 0 mg are reported with 95% confidence intervals.

## RANO Responses

Ordinal RANO outcomes (e.g., pseudoprogression, disease state, best overall response) are analyzed using proportional odds models by visit. Odds ratios versus 0 mg with 95% confidence intervals are reported.

## Time-to-Event Endpoints

Kaplan-Meier curves are presented by dose with risk tables. Overall log-rank p-values are reported for each endpoint. Cox proportional hazards models provide hazard ratios (HRs) for each active dose versus 0 mg with 95% confidence intervals.

# Safety Analyses

Safety summaries include adverse events (AEs), serious adverse events (SAEs), and treatment-emergent adverse events (TEAEs) for the recurrent glioblastoma cohort. AEs are summarized by system organ class and preferred term using MedDRA coding.

# Protocol Deviations

Protocol deviations will be identified prior to database lock and summarized by category and frequency. Major deviations that impact the primary endpoint will be listed and considered in per-protocol sensitivity analyses.

# Interim Analyses

Any interim analyses will be described in the protocol and documented here if conducted. No interim efficacy analyses are planned for the recurrent glioblastoma cohort unless specified in protocol amendments.

# Changes to Protocol-Planned Analyses

There are no changes to the analyses described in the protocol at the time of SAP finalization. Any deviations will be documented here.

# References

- ImPRESS Protocol v7.0 (17-Dec-2024)
- TransCelerate SAP template (CoreBWE)

# Appendices

## Tables, Figures, and Listings

Placeholder: Reference to TFL shells or separate document.
